Plot Historical Decomposition

Source: R/fplot_histdec.R
fplot_histdec.Rd

Plots the historical decomposition for multiple variables, showing the contribution of each structural shock as individual stacked areas, plus a total line overlay. Mirrors the MATLAB Bloom replication figure with facets per variable.

Usage

fplot_histdec(
  histdec_list,
  shock,
  shockname,
  shocknames = NULL,
  dates = NULL,
  p = 0,
  return_data = FALSE,
  facet_ncol = 2,
  facet_scales = c("free", "free_y", "free_x", "fixed"),
  shock_fill = "#407EC9",
  other_fills = "#E87722",
  total_color = "#1a1a1a",
  total_linewidth = 0.8,
  area_alpha = 1
)

Arguments

histdec_list

Named list of (T-p) x N matrices, one per variable to plot. Each element is the $histdec output of fhistdec() called for that variable. Names are used as facet labels.

shock

Integer (1-indexed) selecting the primary shock of interest.

shockname

Character string label for the selected shock (legend entry).

shocknames

Optional character vector of length N with names for all shocks. When supplied the non-selected shocks are shown as individually named stacked areas instead of a single "Residual" bar. Default NULL (falls back to "Residual").

dates

Optional vector of dates (Date, numeric, or integer). Can be the full T-length vector (trimmed internally using p) or the already-trimmed (T-p)-length vector. Default NULL uses an integer index.

p

Integer lag order of the VAR. Used only when dates has length T to trim to dates[(p+1):T]. Default 0.

return_data

Logical. If TRUE returns the long-format tibble instead of the plot. Default FALSE.

facet_ncol

Number of columns in the facet grid. Default 2.

facet_scales

Scale option for facet_wrap: one of "free" (default), "free_y", "free_x", or "fixed".

shock_fill

Fill color for the primary shock area (default: "#407EC9").

other_fills

Character vector of fill colors for the remaining shocks. Recycled if shorter than N - 1. Default "#E87722" (single color used for the "Residual" bar or cycled across named other shocks).

total_color

Color for the total line overlay (default: "#1a1a1a").

total_linewidth

Line width for the total line (default: 0.8).

area_alpha

Transparency for the stacked areas (default: 1).

Value

A ggplot object or a tibble (if return_data = TRUE) with columns date, variable, series, value.

Details

When shocknames is NULL all non-selected shocks are summed into a single "Residual" bar (original behaviour, suitable for the Bloom replication). When shocknames is supplied each shock gets its own named stacked area — useful for the BQ case where the second shock has a meaningful label (e.g. "Non-Technology").

See also

fhistdec

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# BQ case — name both shocks
fplot_histdec(
    histdec_list = histdec_all,
    shock        = 1,
    shockname    = "Technology",
    shocknames   = c("Technology", "Non-Technology"),
    dates        = dates,
    p            = p,
    facet_ncol   = 1
)

# Cholesky case — collapse others into "Residual" (default)
fplot_histdec(
    histdec_list = histdec_list,
    shock        = shock,
    shockname    = "Uncertainty",
    dates        = dates_vec,
    p            = 12
)
} # }
